title: Numerical Computing, Arrays, Visualization, and Reproducibility · Retrieval Circuit
summary: Implement and test reusable computational models over real datasets. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a tested numerical Python notebook and module after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain floating-point approximation and compare values with justified tolerances.
Use NumPy arrays, shapes, dtypes, indexing, and vectorized operations intentionally.
Choose visual encodings, scales, labels, and uncertainty displays that support the question.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper
